verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Mechanisms + schedules documented; Desktop-breaking pkgs identified; 2026-07-21 incident referenced; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the map absent (3/3 failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.mechanism_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/os_update_lifecycle_map.json maps five mechanisms with triggers and observed schedules: unattended-upgrades (apt-daily-upgrade.timer, daily ~06:58, both APT::Periodic flags live-verified ON), apt-daily list refresh (~17:36), the aptdaemon/update-manager interactive path, snap refresh (with its unprivileged observation boundary recorded), and the kernel-install reboot-forcing channel.
gate_result.package_evidence: Five package classes identified with reasons: kernel images (running-kernel removal), snapd, accountsservice, chrome/vscode in-place replacements, and the gnome/gdm session stack — the first four straight from the incident evidence.
gate_result.incident_evidence: The map ties the 2026-07-21 incident causally to the observed mechanism: apt-daily-upgrade.timer LAST run 06:29:49 matches the 06:31 kernel swap to the minute, and the 19:44 aptdaemon batch is the desktop destabilizer; cross-referenced to the failure catalog (FM-01/FM-02) with governance handoffs named for ARCHBP-110..117.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The gate live-reads the real host apt periodic config and asserts the map's claims against it.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-109 is complete: the host's update mechanisms are mapped with observed triggers and timing — unattended-upgrades proven live-enabled and causally tied to the 2026-07-21 kernel swap via the timer's own last-run timestamp — desktop-breaking package classes identified from incident evidence, and governance handoffs named for the remaining T9 lanes.
